productStatement: GrantLayer turns agentic grant workflows into verifiable institutional records.

architectureLayers.name: API / Contract Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: OpenAPI-documented HTTP surface that integrators and clients rely on.

architectureLayers.name: Product Core Domain Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Canonical domain models, lifecycles, and evaluation logic.

architectureLayers.name: Evidence and Verification Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Evidence bundles, hash computation, tamper detection, and completeness scoring.

architectureLayers.name: Policy and Permission Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Policy rule packs, permission profiles, approval lifecycles, and requirement evaluation.

architectureLayers.name: Provenance, Audit, and Export Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Decision provenance, audit event records, and auditor exports.

architectureLayers.name: Persistence Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Durable storage for grants, requests, evidence, audit events, and configuration.

architectureLayers.name: Runtime Configuration Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Environment-specific settings, feature flags, and deployment parameters.

architectureLayers.name: Extension / Adapter Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Pluggable surfaces that map external formats, identity providers, and storage backends into the Product Core without redefining it.

architectureLayers.name: Optional Integration Layer
architectureLayers.purpose: Blockchain anchoring, wallet signing, payment integrations, dashboard clients, and observability sinks that may be attached but are not required for Product Core operation.

coreModules.name: Grant Requests
coreModules.description: Subject-initiated requests for scoped actions, time-bounded and auditable.

coreModules.name: Approvals
coreModules.description: Operator or policy-driven approval decisions linked to grant requests.

coreModules.name: Grants
coreModules.description: Active permission records with Ed25519 signatures and SHA-256 payload hashes.

coreModules.name: Grant Executions
coreModules.description: One record per protected action attempt, linked to a Grant and a Grant Request.

coreModules.name: Evidence Bundles
coreModules.description: Flat, deterministic JSON bundles aggregating the full lifecycle for a single execution.

coreModules.name: Evidence Persistence
coreModules.description: Durable, immutable storage for evidence bundles with hash-based lookup.

coreModules.name: Evidence Verification
coreModules.description: Offline recomputation and comparison of evidence hashes to detect tampering or corruption.

coreModules.name: Evidence Completeness
coreModules.description: Structured score and readiness flag derived from execution, evidence, verification, provenance, and policy coverage.

coreModules.name: Compliance Gap Reports
coreModules.description: Automated gap detection mapped to severity catalogues with recommended actions.

coreModules.name: Policy Requirements / Rule Packs
coreModules.description: Machine-readable policy evaluation with required evidence, exclusions, deadlines, amount limits, required roles, and approval policies.

coreModules.name: Agent Permissions
coreModules.description: Scope-based permission evaluation, permission profiles, and assignment resolution for agents.

coreModules.name: Approval Lifecycle
coreModules.description: Evaluate whether an action needs approval, build approval lifecycles, and transition them through states.

coreModules.name: Decision Provenance
coreModules.description: Structured decision records linking evidence completeness, compliance gaps, permissions, approvals, provenance events, auditor findings, and policy results.

coreModules.name: Auditor Exports
coreModules.description: Institutional auditor export combining all signals into a single structured record with section coverage, blockers, and audit-readiness status.

coreModules.name: Compliance Readiness
coreModules.description: Composite readiness summary across evidence, compliance, permission, approval, provenance, auditor, and policy dimensions.

coreModules.name: API / OpenAPI Contract
coreModules.description: Versioned HTTP contract with stable paths, schemas, and compatibility expectations.

coreModules.name: Audit / Event Records
coreModules.description: Append-only event log capturing significant state changes with timestamps and actor references.

coreModules.name: Persistence Layer
coreModules.description: The abstraction boundary for durable storage, currently implemented with SQLite and optional PostgreSQL.

coreModules.name: Operator / Admin Access Boundary
coreModules.description: The role-based access model that ensures only authorized actors can mutate Product Core state.

stablePublicContracts: Persistent IDs across workflow, grant, execution, evidence, and subject boundaries

extensionSurfaces.name: Policy rule packs
extensionSurfaces.description: Custom rule definitions that feed into the standard policy evaluation contract.

extensionSurfaces.name: Evidence source adapters
extensionSurfaces.description: External evidence feeds mapped to the canonical evidence bundle shape.

extensionSurfaces.name: Evidence verification strategies
extensionSurfaces.description: Alternative or additional verification methods that still produce a standard verification result record.

extensionSurfaces.name: Auditor export formats
extensionSurfaces.description: New output formats that still export the canonical auditor export record structure.

extensionSurfaces.name: Compliance readiness dimensions
extensionSurfaces.description: Additional readiness dimensions that feed into the standard readiness summary.

extensionSurfaces.name: Permission profiles
extensionSurfaces.description: Optional profile definitions evaluated within the standard permission contract.

extensionSurfaces.name: Approval workflow profiles
extensionSurfaces.description: Optional workflow patterns that still produce standard approval lifecycle records.

extensionSurfaces.name: Storage / persistence backends
extensionSurfaces.description: Replaceable storage implementations that preserve Product Core IDs and integrity constraints.

extensionSurfaces.name: Auth / identity providers
extensionSurfaces.description: Replaceable identity and authentication backends that still produce standard operator references and role assignments.

extensionSurfaces.name: SDK / API clients
extensionSurfaces.description: Generated or handwritten clients that speak the stable API contract.

extensionSurfaces.name: Workflow orchestrator integrations
extensionSurfaces.description: External orchestrators that interact via the stable API contract.

extensionSurfaces.name: Observability sinks
extensionSurfaces.description: Metrics, logging, and alerting backends that consume standard event streams.

extensionSurfaces.name: Optional blockchain anchoring
extensionSurfaces.description: Periodic anchoring of integrity hashes to a blockchain for enhanced tamper evidence.

extensionSurfaces.name: Optional wallet / payment integration
extensionSurfaces.description: Wallet signatures, disbursement records, or payment receipts linked to grant executions via stable IDs.

extensionSurfaces.name: Dashboard / UI layer
extensionSurfaces.description: Client applications that consume the stable API contract without embedding Product Core logic.

internalImplementationDetails: Internal file layout and module organization
Low-level helper function names and signatures
Exact storage implementation (schema, indices, migration ordering)
Temporary demo defaults and synthetic data
Non-production auth shortcuts (e.g., admin-token bypass paths)

customizationModel.name: Evidence requirements
customizationModel.scope: what counts as sufficient evidence per domain

customizationModel.name: Export formats
customizationModel.scope: layout and serialization of auditor exports

customizationModel.name: Storage backend
customizationModel.scope: SQLite, PostgreSQL, or future backend via the persistence boundary

customizationModel.name: Deployment / runtime configuration
customizationModel.scope: environment variables, feature flags, container layout

recommendedEvolutionOrder: Freeze and validate API / OpenAPI contract boundaries
Define the auth / operator access model
Add SDK / client examples after contract stabilization
Add UI / dashboard only after API and extension boundaries stabilize
Keep blockchain / payment optional and outside Product Core
